rulings.Case Title: MAXIM GRANT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 8, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT FOOTHILL HARDWARE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE OBJECTION TO THE AY DECLARATION AND THE TAVARES DECLARATION. =(302/PJM/X-MS)

rulings.Case Title: TAYLOR KOROBOW VS. SUSAN HARDIN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 8, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT THE LANSING GROUP, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO CONSIDER EVIDENCE NOT SET FORTH IN MOVANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT. SAN DIEGO WATER CRAFTS, INC. V WELLS FARGO BANK (2002) 102 CAL. APP. 4TH 308, 315 - 316. PLAINTIFF WAS APPRISED OF THE FACTS AT ISSUE BASED ON THE FILING OF THE OTHER DEFENDANTS AND PLAINTIFF ADDRESSED THOSE FACTS. THE ISSUE AS TO THE LANSING GROUP, L.P. WAS IDENTICAL TO THE ISSUE PRESENTED BY THE OTHER DEFENDANTS - DID THE EVIDENCE REFLECT A PATENT OR LATENT DEFECT. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LOCKHART HOWARD et al VS. DANNY'S CONSTRUCTION COMPANY INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 8, 2008 LINE 3. INTERVENOR ARCH INSURANCE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. RANDALL'S CONDUCT WAS AN INDEPENDENT INTERVENING ACT. NEITHER RANDALL'S ACT NOR THE INJURY WERE FORSEEABLE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JIM LASTRA et al VS. HOTEL SUNRISE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 8, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANTS HOTEL SUNRISE L.L.C., KIRIT PATEL AND DHARMESH PATEL'S MOTION For Judgment On The Pleadings: THE COURT DECLINES TO RULE ON THE MOTION. IN FILING THE MOTION, DEFENDANTS SOUGHT PRIMARILY TO CHALLENGE THE CLASS ALLEGATIONS OF THE FOURTEENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS HAVE DISMISSED THOSE ALLEGATIONS. THE COURT WOULD ADDRESS THE COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL ISSUE WERE IT LIMITED TO THE ALLEGED FRAUD. HOWEVER, IT ALSO INCLUDES HABITABILITY AND THAT ISSUE APPEARS TO BE A PART OF SEVERAL OF THE PLAINTIFF'S OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. FOR THAT REASON, THE TRIAL JUDGE WILL BE IN A FAR BETTER POSITION TO DETERMINE WHAT SHOULD AND SHOULD NOT BE TRIED. =(302/PJM/VC)
